summary: Frontend regression pass after removing hardcoded product/category arrays from cafeData.js. Homepage loads cleanly, categories (15) and bestsellers (6) are fetched from the API, all supporting sections render, add-to-cart toasts show correct copy, and live PATCH price update reflects on the UI. Price reverted to 60.0.
passed: GET / homepage loads with no page errors and no DOM error nodes
data-testid=categories-section renders exactly 15 category cards (from GET /api/categories)
data-testid=bestsellers-section renders exactly 6 product cards (from GET /api/products?best_seller=true)
hero-section, gallery-section, reviews-section, contact-section, navbar and footer all render (BRAND/HERO_IMAGE/GALLERY/REVIEWS still imported from cafeData)
Hill Filter Coffee Add toast contains '₹60 · Small'
Wild Hill Honey Add toast contains 'Final price will be confirmed before pickup.'
Live update sanity: PATCH /api/products/hill-filter-coffee price=55.0 → card shows ₹55; reverted to 60.0 (HTTP 200, GET confirms price=60.0)

success_rate.backend: not tested (deferred, only 1 PATCH+GET live-update check ran, both 200)

notes: Only 1 non-critical console line captured ('Found 1 images') — informational, not an error. No import errors after cafeData.js cleanup. Hill Filter Coffee price restored to 60.0 as required.
